annotations.label: Numerical analysis

anchor.snippet: Numerical analysis is the study of algorithms for the problems of continuous mathematics

annotations.note: A meta-definition of the discipline, not a mathematical statement formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Babylonian approximation of √2

anchor.snippet: gives a sexagesimal numerical approximation of the square root of 2

annotations.note: Mathlib has Real.sqrt and irrationality of √2 but no Babylonian/Heron iterative approximation algorithm.

annotations.label: Direct methods

anchor.snippet: Direct methods compute the solution to a problem in a finite number of steps

annotations.note: An informal taxonomy of algorithm types, not a formalizable proposition.

annotations.label: Iterative methods

anchor.snippet: iterative methods are not expected to terminate in a finite number of steps

annotations.note: An informal taxonomy of algorithm types, not a formalizable proposition.

annotations.label: Bisection method for 3x³+4=28

anchor.snippet: As an example, consider the problem of solving

annotations.note: The intermediate value theorem guarantees the root that bisection converges to, but the bisection algorithm itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ill-conditioned problem

anchor.snippet: Ill-conditioned problem: Take the function

annotations.note: Conditioning/condition-number analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Well-conditioned problem

anchor.snippet: Well-conditioned problem: By contrast, evaluating the same function

annotations.note: Conditioning/condition-number analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discretization

anchor.snippet: this process is called ' discretization '

annotations.note: The numerical-analysis notion of discretizing a continuous problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Round-off errors

anchor.snippet: Round-off errors arise because it is impossible to represent all real numbers exactly

annotations.note: Mathlib has no floating-point representation or round-off error analysis.

annotations.label: Truncation errors

anchor.snippet: Truncation errors are committed when an iterative method is terminated or a mathematical procedure is approximated

annotations.note: The general notion of truncation error is not formalized as a definition.

annotations.label: Discretization error

anchor.snippet: discretization induces a discretization error

annotations.note: Discretization error is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Truncation error of bisection example

anchor.snippet: the calculated root is roughly 1.99

annotations.note: A specific worked numerical error estimate that is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Numerical stability

anchor.snippet: An algorithm is called numerically stable if an error

annotations.note: Numerical stability of algorithms is not a concept formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interpolation of temperature

anchor.snippet: Observing that the temperature varies from 20 degrees Celsius

annotations.note: A specific worked interpolation example not present in Mathlib (though general Lagrange interpolation exists).

annotations.label: Extrapolation of GDP

anchor.snippet: If the gross domestic product of a country has been growing an average of 5% per year

annotations.note: A specific worked extrapolation example not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear regression

anchor.snippet: In linear regression, given n points, a line is computed

annotations.note: Least-squares linear regression is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lemonade stand optimization

anchor.snippet: Suppose lemonade is sold at a lemonade stand

annotations.note: A toy applied optimization example not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler method for feather in wind

anchor.snippet: If 100 fans are set up to blow air from one end of the room

annotations.note: Mathlib has ODE existence theory but no formalization of Euler's method.

annotations.label: Horner scheme efficiency

anchor.snippet: a better approach is using the Horner scheme

annotations.note: Mathlib provides a Horner-form recursion principle for polynomials, but no theorem about the scheme's evaluation efficiency.

annotations.label: Interpolation

anchor.snippet: Interpolation solves the following problem

annotations.note: Lagrange.interpolate constructs the polynomial passing through given node values (eval_interpolate_at_node), directly solving the interpolation problem.

annotations.label: Extrapolation

anchor.snippet: Extrapolation is very similar to interpolation

annotations.note: Extrapolation as a numerical-analysis concept is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regression

anchor.snippet: Regression is also similar, but it takes into account that the data are imprecise

annotations.note: Statistical regression / curve fitting is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear vs nonlinear equations

anchor.section: Solving equations and systems of equations
anchor.snippet: Two cases are commonly distinguished, depending on whether the equation is linear or not

annotations.note: This is an informal classification of equation types, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Root-finding algorithms

anchor.section: Solving equations and systems of equations
anchor.snippet: Root-finding algorithms are used to solve nonlinear equations

annotations.note: Mathlib establishes existence of roots via the intermediate value theorem but does not formalize root-finding algorithms themselves.

annotations.label: Optimization problems

anchor.snippet: Optimization problems ask for the point at which a given function is maximized

annotations.note: Mathlib defines maximizing/minimizing points (IsMaxOn/IsMinOn) but does not formalize optimization as an algorithmic problem.

annotations.label: Linear programming

anchor.snippet: linear programming deals with the case that both the objective function and the constraints are linear

annotations.note: Only an internal simplex-algorithm oracle for the linarith tactic exists; linear programming theory (duality, optima) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Method of Lagrange multipliers

anchor.snippet: The method of Lagrange multipliers can be used to reduce optimization problems with constraints

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Lagrange multipliers theorem for constrained local extrema.

annotations.label: Numerical integration

anchor.snippet: Numerical integration, in some instances also known as numerical quadrature

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the trapezoidal rule with its error bound (trapezoidal_error_le), one specific quadrature method rather than the general framework.

annotations.label: Solving PDEs by discretization

anchor.snippet: Partial differential equations are solved by first discretizing the equation

annotations.note: Numerical PDE discretization methods are not formalized in Mathlib.
